info.description:
Go + PocketBase backend for QuantumAtlas. Read endpoints
(wiki/pages/stats/search/graph metadata/health) are public
because the wiki is an open repo; write endpoints require a
bearer token (PAT or PocketBase session) plus the matching
scope. See the auth model docs for the scope vocabulary.
info.title: QuantumAtlas API

post.description: Executes caller-supplied read-only Cypher. graph:read scope.

get.description:
Returns 200 with {"error":...} when Neo4j is unreachable
(the UI renders a friendly banner rather than a crash page).
Requires the graph:read scope.

Liveness plus parallel dependency probes (rawstore, neo4j,
wiki). HTTP status is always 200; read data.status for the
real verdict ("healthy" | "degraded").

post.description:
Session-only (PAT auth rejected). Marks the pending request
as approved so the next /token poll mints the PAT bound to
the approving user.

get.description:
Session-only (PAT auth rejected). Returns the PAT spec
(name, description, scopes, expires_in_days) tied to a
pending user_code so the /<lang>/device page can show the
user what they're about to approve.

parameters.description: short user code (with or without dashes)
parameters.name: user_code

post.description:
RFC 8628 §3.1. Anonymous (no auth) — the CLI uses this to
obtain a device_code (kept secret, polled on /token) and a
short user_code that the human enters on the SPA's
/<lang>/device page after authenticating.

parameters.description: PAT spec to mint after approval
parameters.name: body

post.description:
Session-only (PAT auth rejected). Marks the pending request
as denied so the next /token poll returns "access_denied".

RFC 8628 §3.4 + §3.5. Anonymous. The CLI polls this with
device_code at the published interval until the server
returns the minted PAT plaintext (success) or an error
string ("authorization_pending", "slow_down", "expired_token",
"access_denied", "invalid_grant"). HTTP status is always
400 on errors per the RFC so the CLI can switch on `error`.
post.summary: Poll for PAT after device approval

get.description:
Lists wiki entries. Source pages (processed papers) are
treated as citations and excluded unless page_type=source.

Counts of PDFs, markdown, JSON and mineru-pending papers from
the S3 paper index. Returns {available:false} when no S3
backend is configured.

201.description: claim granted (body is the claim record)

404.description: not claimable (no PDF in catalog, or markdown already exists)

409.description: already claimed by someone else (body includes existing claim details)
claim_id does not match the active claim

post.description:
Accepts the entire MinerU result zip exactly as returned by `full_zip_url`. Server extracts `full.md` plus every `images/*` entry and stores them to the markdown and images object buckets respectively. Images are written before the markdown so any reader that observes the markdown also observes all referenced images. Replaces the v0.7.x `upload-markdown` endpoint (which only accepted a single .md file and silently dropped images).

The {arxiv_id} slot also accepts a DOI (`10.<registrant>/<suffix>`) to contribute the converted *published* version. DOI bundles are stored under the `markdown/doi/...` + `images/doi/...` namespace; the server resolves canonical metadata (title, authors, linked arxiv id) from OpenAlex on the contributor's behalf — there is no `title` / `authors` form field. `verify=strict` rejects when OpenAlex cannot resolve the DOI (see upload-pdf). The result is reported in `X-QAtlas-Verification`.
post.summary: Upload paper MinerU bundle (arXiv id or DOI)

parameters.description: sha256 of the source PDF that was converted (cross-checked against stored PDF)
parameters.name: pdf_sha256

parameters.description: DOI only: 'strict' rejects when OpenAlex cannot resolve the DOI (default warn)
parameters.name: verify

parameters.description: short label of the contributor's MinerU run (truncated to 64 chars)
parameters.name: source

parameters.description: MinerU result zip (must contain full.md; optional images/*)
parameters.name: mineru_zip

post.description:
Content-addressed upload with sha256 idempotency. 200 when
bytes are unchanged, 201 when written, 409 on a content
conflict without overwrite=true.

The {arxiv_id} slot also accepts a DOI (`10.<registrant>/<suffix>`)
for contributing a *published* version that may have no arXiv
preprint. DOI uploads are stored under a disjoint `pdf/doi/...`
namespace and the server resolves the DOI's title / authors /
linked arxiv id from OpenAlex — the contributor cannot supply
that metadata. The result is reported in `X-QAtlas-Verification`
and the JSON `verification` block; `verify=strict` rejects a
doi-not-found with 409 (or metadata-unavailable / unconfigured
with 503).
post.summary: Upload paper PDF (arXiv id or DOI)

parameters.description: DOI only: 'strict' rejects when OpenAlex cannot resolve the DOI (default warn)
parameters.name: verify

get.description:
Returns the cached MinerU markdown for the given arxiv id
(or DOI — the id_or_doi path component is auto-detected
against the IANA prefix `10.<registrant>/...`). Only
registered when QATLAS_PAPER_ACCESS_ENABLED=true on the
server (default off).

Canonical resolution: a `:PaperWork` node with
`identifier_scheme='doi'` ALWAYS wins over its arxiv
twin when both exist — DOI is the canonical identity of
the published version. The dispatcher serves DOI bytes
for either id form when a DOI contribution is on file;
pass `?force_arxiv=1` to opt out per request (DOI input
with force_arxiv + no arxiv twin returns 409). See
docs/reference/upload-api.md §Canonical resolution.

Long-running operation semantics: on cache miss the
server may transparently fetch the PDF from arxiv.org
(silent_fetch) and trigger a MinerU conversion. The
first call returns 202 with `Operation-Location:
/api/papers/{id}/markdown/status` and `Retry-After: 5`;
clients poll the status endpoint until state=cached then
re-GET this resource for the bytes.

parameters.description: arXiv canonical id with vN suffix, or a DOI (e.g. 10.1103/PhysRevLett.103.150502)
parameters.name: id_or_doi

parameters.description: 1/true: bypass the DOI-canonical default and serve the arxiv twin (or 409 if no twin exists)
parameters.name: force_arxiv

404.description: DOI not in OpenAlex / not on arxiv; or paper unknown and silent fetch unavailable

409.description: force_arxiv requested but DOI has no arxiv twin

502.description: prior conversion failed inside the cooldown window, or OpenAlex upstream error

503.description: cache-only mode (no MinerU keys), or DOI resolution unavailable (QATLAS_OPENALEX_MAILTO unset)

get.description:
Side-effect-free poll surface. Never starts a job and
never triggers a fetch. Only registered when
QATLAS_PAPER_ACCESS_ENABLED=true on the server.

Canonical resolution: same DOI-wins rule as
/api/papers/{id_or_doi}/markdown. Pass `?force_arxiv=1`
to query the arxiv-side status instead.

Response shape always carries the agent-decision triple
`state / pdf_ready / md_ready` plus an optional `phase`
(fetching_pdf | converting_md | ready | error_fetching |
error_converting) and `fetch` / `convert` sub-objects
with bytes_received / mineru_task_id / polled_count so a
polling client can show precise progress.

404.description: paper unknown and silent fetch unavailable

get.description:
Returns the cached PDF (application/pdf) for the given
arxiv id or DOI. Only registered when
QATLAS_PAPER_ACCESS_ENABLED=true on the server.

Canonical resolution: a DOI contribution ALWAYS wins
over its arxiv twin when both exist — the dispatcher
serves the DOI PDF for either id form. Pass
`?force_arxiv=1` to opt out per request (DOI input
without an arxiv twin then returns 409). See
docs/reference/upload-api.md §Canonical resolution.

Long-running operation semantics mirror /markdown: cache
miss returns 202 with Operation-Location pointing at
/pdf/status. The fetch path uses a separate semaphore
from MinerU conversion (QATLAS_ARXIV_FETCH_CONCURRENT)
and a polite-pool rate limiter (QATLAS_ARXIV_FETCH_RPS).

parameters.description: arXiv canonical id with vN suffix, or a DOI
parameters.name: id_or_doi

parameters.description: 1/true: bypass DOI-canonical default; return 409 if DOI has no arxiv twin
parameters.name: force_arxiv

404.description: arxiv 404 or DOI not on arxiv

409.description: force_arxiv requested but DOI has no arxiv twin

502.description: arxiv upstream error / OpenAlex upstream error

503.description: silent fetch disabled (no fetcher), DOI resolution unavailable

get.description:
Side-effect-free poll surface for /pdf. Same shape as
/markdown/status but states are restricted to the
fetch-only flow — no convert phase. Only registered when
QATLAS_PAPER_ACCESS_ENABLED=true.

Canonical resolution: same DOI-wins rule as
/api/papers/{id_or_doi}/pdf. Pass `?force_arxiv=1` to
query the arxiv-side status instead.

post.description:
Mints a personal access token. Plaintext is returned exactly
once. Requires a PocketBase session token (PAT auth is
refused here, to stop a leaked PAT from self-replicating).

get.description:
Lists the available scopes and their descriptions so clients
can render the create form without hardcoding the vocabulary.

post.description:
Runs `git fetch --prune` + `git pull --ff-only` on the server
wiki checkout, then refreshes the in-memory cache. Mutates
server state, so it requires the wiki:write scope.

get.description:
Branch/commit/ahead/behind of the server's wiki checkout.
Requires the wiki:read scope (the knowledge base is not
anonymously readable).

Returns a POSIX sh script (text/x-shellscript) that downloads
the latest qatlasd release binary.

scopes.description: REQUIRED: may be empty for "no permissions"

BearerAuth.description:
"Bearer <token>" — two credential shapes
accepted: a Personal Access Token
(`Authorization: Bearer qat_...`, minted at
`/pat` after GitHub OAuth login), or the
env-loaded system PAT (set
`QATLAS_SYSTEM_PAT` on the server, send the
plaintext as `Authorization: Bearer <value>`).
Browser callers are authenticated through
pb.authStore (no copy step) — only non-browser
callers need an explicit bearer.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
